dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4461 IS: Visual Protection of Strategic Assets Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend chapter 37 of title 18, United States Code, to improve the prohibition on espionage.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Visual Protection of Strategic Assets Act.

section.header: Improvements to the prohibition on espionage

subsection.header: Gathering, transmitting, or losing defense information
subsection.text: Section 793 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: the term country of concern means—

clause.text: the Democratic People's Republic of Korea;

subparagraph.text: the term covered person means—

clause.text: a citizen or national of a country of concern; or
a person acting on behalf of a citizen or national of a country of concern;

subparagraph.text: the term high-value asset means an asset included on the list maintained under paragraph (3); and
the term tier-1 installation means a military installation designated as a tier-1 installation by the Secretary of Defense under paragraph (4).

For purposes of this section, there shall be a rebuttable presumption that a covered person who photographs, videos, or tracks a high-value asset or tier-1 installation photographed, videoed, or tracked the high-value asset or tier-1 installation with intent or reason to believe the information would injure the United States or advantage a foreign nation.

A covered person may rebut the presumption under subparagraph (A) by demonstrating, by clear and convincing evidence, that the covered person photographed, videoed, or tracked the high-value asset or tier-1 installation with prior approval from the Secretary of Defense.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Defense shall maintain a list of high-value assets of the Department of Defense, which shall include—

subparagraph.text: the Boeing E–4B Nightwatch aircraft;
the Boeing RC–135 aircraft;
the Northrop B–2 Spirit aircraft;
the Rockwell B–1 Lancer aircraft; and
any nuclear command platform.

paragraph.text: The Secretary of Defense, in consultation with the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff and the Secretaries of the military departments, shall designate as a tier-1 installation each military installation that—

subparagraph.text: houses, supports, or serves as a primary operating location for 1 or more high-value assets; or
is critical to the execution of national strategic missions, including nuclear deterrence, global strike, or strategic intelligence collection.
not less frequently than annually, make publicly available an updated list of high-value assets and tier-1 installations; and
install signs and other markers—

clause.text: identifying high-value assets and tier-1 installations; and
stating that photography and other surveillance of the high-value assets and tier-1 installations are strictly prohibited.

subsection.header: Modernizing and strengthening the prohibition on photographing and sketching defense installations

paragraph.text: Section 795 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subsection (a), by striking photograph, and inserting photograph (including a digital photograph), video (including the transmission of video content over the internet contemporaneously with, or nearly contemporaneously with, the capturing of the video content), visual depiction enhanced using artificial intelligence (as defined in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401)),; and
by striking subsection (b) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the terms country of concern, high-value asset, and tier-1 installation have the meanings given such terms in section 793(i)(1).

subparagraph.text: shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 1 year, or both;
if the violation involved a high-value asset or a tier-1 installation, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 7 years, or both; or
if the defendant is a citizen or national of a country of concern, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not less than 5 years and not more than 10 years, or both.

subsection.text: Any person who violates this section shall—

paragraph.text: forfeit to the United States, irrespective of any provision of State law, any electronic or other device used in the commission of the violation; and
be subject to a civil penalty of not more than $100,000.

Any citizen or national of a country of concern (as defined in section 793(i)(1) of title 18, United States Code), as added by subsection (a), who has been convicted of a violation described in section 795 of title 18, United States Code, as amended by paragraph (1), shall—

subparagraph.text: have his or her visa revoked, in accordance with section 221(i) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1201(i)); and
shall be placed in removal proceedings, in accordance with sections 239 and 240 of such Act (8 U.S.C. 1229 and 1229a).
